summary: Large gmresultplayer decompilation/matching pass for the results screen. The PR replaced many placeholders in src/melee/gm/gmresultplayer.c with typed C, added/updated prototypes and result data bitfields, and adjusted GALE01 symbols for newly understood data/table boundaries. decomp.dev reported +1772 matched bytes, 5 new full matches, and 14 improved unmatched items; one fn_8017A9B4 regression was explicitly accepted because the rewrite cleaned up pointer math and left mostly regswaps.
key_files: src/melee/gm/gmresultplayer.c (+1299/-56): main results-player decomp pass, replacing placeholders for fn_80178050, fn_801785B0, fn_80178BB4, fn_801791E4, fn_80179350, fn_801795D4, fn_801796F0, fn_80179854, fn_80179990, fn_8017A078, fn_8017A318, fn_8017A67C, fn_8017AA78 and cleaning fn_80179F84/fn_8017A9B4.
src/melee/gm/gmresultplayer.h: replaced several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete signatures for the newly decompiled functions.
src/melee/gm/types.h: split ResultsPlayerData.x0 and ResultsData.x0 flags into bitfields and exposed ResultsData.x3.
src/melee/gm/gmresult.c and src/melee/gm/gmresult.h: changed fn_80174A60 return type from u8 to s32 and rewrote results-player scroll/page flags through bitfields.
config/GALE01/symbols.txt: adjusted data/sdata symbol extents, including splitting lbl_803D6A18/lbl_803D6A28 and coalescing lbl_804D3FD4 into a 0x20 table-like object.
what_changed: Added local typed layouts for previously raw results-player globals/data, including CharScaleEntry, ResultsRenderFuncs, ResultsPlayerConfig, CameraKindParams, CameraKindData, lbl_8046E3AC_t, and ResultsDisplayData around lbl_8046E1B0/lbl_8046E3AC.

Implemented results flow logic in fn_80178050 and fn_80179350: animation frame gates, per-slot confirmation/scroll handling, JObj visibility flags, audio triggers, state transitions, and final transition via gm_801A4B60.

Implemented results presentation/setup functions for animations, result banner/texture frames, player/rank/taunt JObjs, background flash, image descriptors, camera CObjs, player model setup, and EFB image capture.
Matched small helper functions according to bot report: fn_801791E4, fn_801796F0, fn_801795D4, fn_80179F84, and fn_80177DD0 reached 100%.

Improved many larger gmresultplayer functions but did not fully match all of them; bot listed fn_80178BB4, fn_801785B0, fn_80178050, fn_8017A67C, fn_80179990, fn_8017A318, fn_80179350, fn_8017A078, fn_8017AA78, and fn_80179854 as improved but still not all 100%.
Replaced manual bitmask writes such as pdata->x0 |= 0x10 and pdata->x0 &= ~0x20 with named bitfields x0_1 through x0_4 in ResultsPlayerData.
Changed fn_80174A60 count handling from u8 to s32, aligning the header and callers with the decompiled usage.
smart_moves: Typed the large lbl_8046E1B0 blob as ResultsDisplayData so image descriptors, GObjs, JObjs, and embedded state fields could be accessed directly instead of raw byte offsets.
Accepted a known matching regression in fn_8017A9B4 to remove opaque pointer math; the review explicitly notes this cleanup was worthwhile because remaining differences were mostly regswaps.
Used bitfields for partially understood flag bytes while preserving offset-style names, giving future work better semantics without overnaming uncertain behavior.
Promoted function signatures in headers as soon as enough call information was known, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS spread and improving type pressure for later decompilation.
Kept narrowly scoped local structs with offset field names such as x24/x30/x3C when full semantic names were not yet justified, avoiding speculative naming.
Documented one odd cast in fn_80177DD0 as permuter slop, making clear that the unsigned long long slot cast is a matching artifact rather than intended API design.
decomp_lessons: For large result/menu globals, first carve the storage into image descriptors, object pointers, copied match data, and per-slot tables; this can unlock many functions that otherwise look like pointer arithmetic soup.

Bitfielding flag bytes can improve both readability and matching, but keep conservative x0_N names until flag meanings are confirmed; comments like confirmed flag or phase are useful where evidence exists.
A small symbol-map boundary correction can matter: splitting/coalescing data objects in symbols.txt should accompany typed tables in C so future references point at the right object sizes.
Clean C that slightly regresses one function may still be a net win if it replaces raw offsets and exposes structure layout that improves neighboring functions.
decomp.dev per-function reports are useful triage: this PR shows a mix of full matches, high-percent improvements, and one accepted regression rather than an all-or-nothing pass.
naming_conventions: Address-based function names fn_8017xxxx were retained; no semantic function names were introduced by this PR.
New local types use PascalCase nouns for inferred layouts: CharScaleEntry, ResultsRenderFuncs, ResultsPlayerConfig, CameraKindParams, CameraKindData, ResultsDisplayData.
Partially understood struct fields keep offset-style names such as x24, x30, x3C, x74, x0_0, x0_23, with comments only where evidence is present.
Known enum/API names were used where available, e.g. Gm_PKind_Human, Gm_PKind_Demo, CKIND_SEAK, CKIND_ZELDA, CKIND_GAMEWATCH.
Per-slot arrays are consistently indexed by slot/arg0/arg2 and kept as fixed-size 4-element tables where the result screen works over player slots.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) and PAD_STACK(16) in several functions to satisfy stack layout/matching constraints.
Used explicit casts to u8, s8, s32, and an unsigned long long slot cast in fn_80177DD0, with a comment that the latter is permuter slop.
Preserved repeated variant clamp expressions like variant <= 2 ? variant : 3 rather than prematurely abstracting them, likely to keep register/codegen behavior.
Used do-while loops and fixed iteration counts over 4 slots or 6 player_data entries to mirror the original control flow.
Used typed static data such as lbl_804D3FD0[4][2] and extern table declarations for lbl_803D7018/lbl_803D7038/lbl_803D6A18/lbl_803D7058 to turn indexed loads into readable table accesses.
For float/data copies, retained bitwise-looking assignments such as *(s32*)&cx = *(s32*)&config->x74 where that shape helped matching.
Introduced fn_80179350_inline as a small wrapper around fn_801791E4, indicating an inlining/codegen tactic rather than a semantic abstraction.
review_feedback: No line review comments were present in the slice.
PR body was minimal: "Tracking PR as usual".
decomp.dev reported matched code 61.45% (+0.05%, +1772 bytes), 5 new matches, 14 improvements, and 1 regression.

Human review note: "Regression is fine since it cleaned up a lot of pointer math and most of the remaining bits are regswaps." This refers to accepting cleanup despite the reported fn_8017A9B4 regression.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename gmresultplayer fn_80178050/fn_80178BB4/fn_8017A67C after this struct groundwork.
Search for uses of ResultsDisplayData or lbl_8046E1B0 to see whether the local struct was promoted to a shared header.
Check subsequent changes to fn_8017A9B4 to see whether the accepted pointer-math cleanup regression was recovered.
Search for additional GALE01 symbols.txt corrections around lbl_803D6A18, lbl_803D6A28, and lbl_804D3FD4 after table typing matured.
